baseline.subject: Add Mira Core Phase 29 runtime dry-run implementation validation

purpose: Define a validation-only contract for the first disabled local dry-run control-path implementation gate after Phase 29.
Specify the smallest future code slice that can begin satisfying Phase 29 prerequisites without enabling runtime.
Keep disabled mode, fail-closed controls, local-only dry-run request/response shapes, operator-visible status, redacted preview telemetry, reference-only dependencies, and proof truth explicit.
nonGoals: No real server, listener, route, HTTP, WebSocket, network, database, store, file, migration, queue, lease, auth, key, secret, env secret read, local execution, shell, PTY, browser, screenshot, OCR, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, or output-file behavior.
No source-store writes and no mutation of Phase 29 artifacts.
No live local-arm execution, model-processing proof, bridge-green proof, or direct server targeting of Builder/Oracle.
builderPatchMustSatisfy: Treat eligible_for_future_disabled_local_control_path_validation as non-authorizing and not runtime proof.
Reject stale baseline 8084b29 or stale Phase 29 dependency.
Define a disabled-by-default local runtime mode flag reader as validation metadata only.
Define a fail-closed kill switch check as validation metadata only.
Define dry-run request/response shapes as deterministic previews only.
Preserve local in-process or loopback-only boundary with no listener/network now.
Emit operator-visible status summary, rollback/disable behavior, redacted telemetry/audit preview, and reference-only config/auth/key/storage refs.
CLI, if implemented later, must be stdout-only and ignore output-file flags.

staticValidationRules.description: Phase 30 contract baseline must stay pinned to 8084b29.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Baseline commit changes or is missing.

staticValidationRules.description: Phase 29 runtime dry-run implementation dependency must point to commit 8084b29 and remain validation-only.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Phase 29 dependency commit, fixture path, module path, status, default decision, or non-authorizing eligible flags are stale.

staticValidationRules.description: Default control-path decision remains contract-only.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Default decision is eligible, runtime-ready, runtime-started, authorized, or live.

staticValidationRules.description: Eligible for future disabled local control-path validation is a future review state only.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Eligible is treated as authorization, runtime proof, execution permission, or side-effect permission.

staticValidationRules.description: Runtime mode flag reader defaults disabled/off and remains dev-only, local-only, operator-visible, non-authorizing, and secret-free.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Flag reader is default-on, hidden, non-local, non-dev, authorizing, runtime-starting, or reads env secrets.

staticValidationRules.description: Kill switch check is required, fail-closed, testable, and cannot be ignored.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Kill switch is missing, default-open, ignored, not testable, authorizing, or checking it performs side effects.

staticValidationRules.description: Dry-run request shape is validation-only, idempotent, redacted payload only, and operator-status aware.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Request shape allows raw payload, non-idempotent input, action payloads, or missing operator-visible status fields.

staticValidationRules.description: Dry-run response shape is status preview only and cannot execute, write, queue, lease, or authorize runtime.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Response shape claims local work, queue/lease, store/file write, send/deploy/trade, or authorization.

staticValidationRules.description: Local boundary remains in-process or loopback-only and opens no listener/network in this phase.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Allowed hosts include public interfaces or the report claims listener, route, port, remote interface, or network activity.

staticValidationRules.description: Operator status summary is visible, status-only, and includes mode, kill switch, boundary, and proof truth.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Operator status authorizes runtime, hides required control state, or reports proof not actually available.

staticValidationRules.description: Rollback/disable behavior is idempotent, non-destructive, and cannot resurrect blocked state.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Rollback deletes data, revives blocked runtime/action state, authorizes runtime, or claims execution now.

staticValidationRules.description: Telemetry/audit is redacted preview only, with sink references only and no writes/raw payload.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Telemetry writes to store/file/db, emits raw payload, or claims a real sink.

staticValidationRules.description: Config/auth/key/storage refs remain reference-only with no raw path/secret and no env secret read.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Output includes raw path, raw secret, token, private/data key, env secret read, or real auth/key/storage operation.

staticValidationRules.description: All Phase 30 control-path gates remain visible as future validation gates and do not satisfy or authorize runtime now.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Any gate is missing, marked satisfied now, marked authorizing, or not tied to a Phase 29 prerequisite.

staticValidationRules.description: Runtime flag reader, kill switch check, dry-run response, control-path presence, socket, and delivery acceptance are not runtime/model/bridge/local-arms proof.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Any proof boundary is flipped true or used to authorize runtime/local execution.

staticValidationRules.description: Server-originated direct Builder/Oracle targets remain blocked; local Architect acceptance remains required.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Builder or Oracle are allowed as direct server targets or local Architect acceptance is bypassed.

staticValidationRules.description: Control-path planning may not drift into customer sends, deploys, trades, money movement, external sends, captures, memory commits, Tier2+, or local execution.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Next safe actions, statuses, decisions, request shape, or response shape authorize unsafe work.

staticValidationRules.description: No runtime/server/listener/routes/network/db/store/file/migration/queue/lease/auth/key/secret/local execution/send/deploy/trade/output-file behavior.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Any side-effect truth field is false or any side-effect counter is non-zero.

staticValidationRules.description: Raw private content, raw paths, secrets, tokens, keys, terminal scrollback, browser state, screenshots, OCR, customer private content, plaintext, ciphertext, decrypted payloads, and proof-overclaim strings are blocked.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Forbidden raw/private/secret/runtime proof substrings appear in output values.

staticValidationRules.description: Idempotency is stable for equivalent inputs and sensitive to baseline, Phase 29 dependency, control path shapes, proof boundaries, gates, and decision changes.
staticValidationRules.failClosedWhen: Equivalent inputs produce different keys or changed inputs reuse a key.

acceptanceChecks.expects: baseline_commit is 8084b29.
phase_29_dependency commit is 8084b29 and status is committed_validation_only.
Phase 29 eligible state remains non-authorizing and not runtime proof.
Default control_path_decision is remain_control_path_contract_only.
eligible_for_future_disabled_local_control_path_validation does not authorize runtime or prove runtime.
Runtime mode flag reader is disabled by default, dev-only, local-only, operator-visible, non-authorizing, and secret-free.
Invalid flag values fail closed to status preview.
Kill switch check is required, fail-closed, testable, default engaged, and cannot be ignored.
Request shape is validation-only, idempotent, redacted payload only, and includes operator-visible status needs.
Response shape is status preview only and cannot execute work, queue/lease, write, or authorize runtime.
Boundary is in-process or loopback-only, with no public/remote interface, listener, route, port, or network now.
Operator status is visible, status-only, and includes mode, kill switch, boundary, and proof truth.
Rollback/disable is idempotent, non-destructive, non-authorizing, and cannot resurrect blocked state.
Telemetry/audit preview is redacted, sink-reference-only, and writes no store/file/db.
Config/auth/key/storage refs are reference-only, with no raw path/secret/env secret read.
All nine Phase 30 gate IDs are present as future control-path validation gates.
Each Phase 30 gate has satisfies_phase29_now=false and authorizes_runtime=false.
Runtime flag reader, kill switch check, dry-run response, control-path presence, socket, and delivery acceptance are not runtime/model/bridge/local-arms proof.
Direct Builder/Oracle server target flags remain false and local Architect acceptance is required.
All no_* side-effect truth fields are true and counters are zero.
Changing baseline 8084b29 fails validation.
Changing Phase 29 dependency commit, status, or default decision fails validation.
Eligible decision with authorization/proof/runtime flags fails validation.
Default-on runtime flag reader, runtime_started=true, or authorizing flag fails validation.
Missing or ignored kill switch check fails validation.
Dry-run request shape that allows raw payload fails validation.
Dry-run response shape that executes work or writes/queues/leases fails validation.
Public/remote interface, listener, route, port, or network overclaim fails validation.
Telemetry/audit store/file/db write or raw payload fails validation.
Raw secret/path/private/token/key/payload leakage fails validation.
Customer send, deploy, trade, money movement, external send, memory commit, capture, or local execution drift fails validation.
Runtime flag reader, kill switch check, dry-run response, control-path, socket, delivery, model-processing, or bridge-green proof overclaim fails validation.
Omitting any Phase 30 gate fails validation.
Marking a Phase 30 gate satisfied now or authorizing runtime fails validation.
Idempotency changes when baseline, dependency, flag reader, kill switch, request/response shape, boundary, proof, or gates change.

tamperCases.mutation: Change phase_29_dependency.commit from 8084b29 to d4aa1a5 or status away from committed_validation_only.
Set local_boundary.allowed_hosts=['0.0.0.0'], public_interface_allowed=true, listener_opened_now=true, or network_performed_now=true.
Insert bearer token, env secret read, raw path, private key, customer private content, plaintext, ciphertext, or decrypted payload into output string values.
Add next safe action or request/response summary that says send email to customer, message the client, approve deploy, move money, external send, capture screen, commit memory, or execute shell.
Remove runtime-mode-flag-reader or reference-only-config-auth-key-storage from phase29_prerequisite_gate_status.
Set any side_effect_result no_* field false or a side-effect counter above zero.

summaryAcceptanceCriteria: Fixture pins Phase 30 baseline 8084b29 and Phase 29 dependency 8084b29.
Fixture defines runtime_control_path_report plus validation_report output shapes.
Default decision is remain_control_path_contract_only; eligible_for_future_disabled_local_control_path_validation is non-authorizing and not runtime proof.
Runtime mode flag reader is disabled-by-default, dev-only, local-only, operator-visible, non-authorizing, fail-closed on invalid values, and secret-free.
Kill switch check is required, fail-closed, testable, default engaged, non-authorizing, and cannot be ignored.
Dry-run request shape is validation-only, idempotent, redacted payload only, and includes operator-visible status needs.
Dry-run response shape is status preview only and cannot execute work, create queue/lease, write store/file, or authorize runtime.
Local boundary is in-process or loopback-only with no public/remote interface, listener, route, port, or network now.
Operator status summary is visible and status-only; rollback/disable behavior is idempotent, non-destructive, non-authorizing, and cannot resurrect blocked state.
Telemetry/audit preview is redacted and sink-reference-only with no raw payload or store/file/db writes.
Config/auth/key/storage refs remain reference-only with no raw path, token, key, secret, or env secret read.
All nine Phase 30 gates appear as future validation gates; none are satisfied now or runtime-authorizing.
Proof boundaries preserve that runtime flag reader, kill switch check, dry-run response, control-path presence, socket, and delivery acceptance are not runtime/model/bridge/local-arms proof.
Direct Builder/Oracle server targets remain blocked and local Architect acceptance remains required.

Static rules and tamper cases cover stale baseline/dependency, eligible-as-authorization, default-on flag reader, missing kill switch check, raw request payload, response execution, non-loopback boundary, telemetry writes, raw leaks, unsafe action drift, fake proof, direct target bypass, missing/satisfied gates, and side-effect lies.
